design_philosophy.core_loop: Recruit party members with customizable classes, explore towns and dungeons, engage in turn-based combat, level up through experience, equip better gear, defeat increasingly powerful foes to reach Baramos
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (HP/MP management in dungeons) versus exploration discovery; player agency in party composition versus narrative progression to Baramos
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Building a personalized adventuring party through recruitment and class customization; being the hero who saves the world from the demon lord Baramos
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical party flexibility—hired mercenaries rather than story characters; class-change system at level 20 allows hybrid builds; open-world structure lets players tackle objectives in varied order; day/night cycle gates certain quests and NPCs; tavern-based recruitment means party is entirely player-constructed

difficulty.checkpointing: SRAM save system allows persistent saves; players can save at towns/churches
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Class selection and party composition directly control encounter difficulty; ability to recruit diverse classes provides multiple tactical approaches; monster arena betting allows gold grinding for better equipment

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling; equipment purchases and loot drops; class-change system at level 20 allowing stat reallocation and hybrid builds; personality system (in remakes) affects stat growth rates
progression.unlock_mechanism: New party recruits unlock at taverns in different towns; class changes unlock at Dhama Temple (halfway through game); day/night cycle unlocks time-specific quests and NPCs; geographical exploration unlocks new regions and shops
replayability_hooks: Multiple viable class combinations and party configurations
Day/night cycle creates different content per location
Monster arena for alternative gold grinding
Multiple personality traits affect character progression differently
Ability to swap party members in/out of active roster

world_building_techniques: Town exploration and NPC dialogue reveals lore and quests
Environmental storytelling through dungeon design and geography
Day/night cycle creates temporal world-building (quests only available at certain times)
Scripted battle with Ortega provides narrative context (unique sprite in NA version)
Demon lord Baramos serves as overarching narrative goal

notable_bosses: Ortega (scripted battle with unique death animation in NA version)

danger_signals: Demon lord Baramos established as world-threatening antagonist
Monster arena concept reflects organized combat culture in towns
environmental_storytelling: Multiple towns with distinct shops, taverns, and NPCs create sense of populated world
Temples (including Dhama Temple and Dharma Temple) serve as class-change and healing centers
Shrines and churches function as religious/healing infrastructure
Cemeteries and graveyards provide gothic atmosphere (visual localization differences show cross→tombstone changes)
